title: lathyrus aureus subsp. occidentalis
intro: Lathyrus aureus subsp. occidentalis, also known as golden pea or western meadowpea, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family that is native to western North America. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as arthritis and muscle spasms. The health benefits of Lathyrus aureus subsp. occidentalis include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to act as a diuretic and to support kidney function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
